quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of casual substance abuse settled over the Leading section, as one anon recounted peer-pressuring a roommate into taking drug hits. This drew a scattered but intense response.

A front of geopolitical tension moved into the Discourse section, as an anon shared an article about an Israel-based company's alleged attempts to interfere in Indian elections.

This was accompanied by a cold snap of brutal descriptions from another anon, whose scenario was described as 'absolutely brutal'.

The board's usual overcast conditions were briefly interrupted by a drizzle of queries about South Indian ancestry and a suggestion to create a /gen/ board, but these quickly burned off without leaving a lasting impact.

articles.title: Anon recounts peer-pressuring roommate into taking drug hits
articles.body:
Anon shared an experience of smoking with friends in a college hostel and peer-pressuring a roommate, described as a 'skinny brahmin kid,' into taking some hits after showing interest. The roommate reportedly believed he was high after taking the hits.

Another anon chimed in with a similar story of inviting a 'skinny Brahmin friend' over for biryani, only to find that his teeth struggled to tear the meat off the bone, describing it as 'pure comedy' and 'absolute humiliation.' One respondent expressed skepticism, calling the initial claim a 'shitty LARP' and suggesting no one's teeth would fall out from eating steak.

A second poster jokingly referenced 'teeth falling off' due to eating 'shitty low-quality boiled and fried meat.' The conversation took a turn with personal attacks and insults.

Anon criticized the board, saying 'this place is worse than xitter.' A separate poster made a claim about having sex with someone's mom. The discussion continued with various exchanges of insults and personal attacks.

Anon mentioned 'sending Allah missiles' and another poster referenced 'sending Al Zutt missiles.' As the conversation progressed, it became increasingly hostile. One anon told another to 'sit down' and accused them of 'baiting.' A later post made a claim about Sonia Gandhi.

The thread was marked by a series of aggressive and mocking exchanges between users.

articles.title: Anon Shares Article on Israel-Based Company's Alleged Attempts to Interfere in Indian Elections
articles.body:
Anon posted a link to an article from The Hindu about OpenAI's claims of stalling attempts by an Israel-based company to interfere in Indian elections. The article is from 2024.

One respondent alleged that the company, which was not named, had infiltrated the Indian government and cited examples of politicians whose phones were allegedly compromised with Pegasus spyware.

The politicians mentioned include Rahul Gandhi, Abhishek Banerjee, Prahlad Singh Patel, Ashok Lavasa, and Ashwini Vaishnaw. Another poster claimed that the company's loyalty was to its customers, implying that it did not discriminate between Indian political parties.

This anon suggested that both the Congress and the BJP were customers of such cyber espionage companies. A different thread of discussion emerged, with one poster accusing Jews of playing a 'double game' and creating both the American left and right wings.

This poster also made a remark about Palestinian people, suggesting they were a Jewish creation. The conversation also touched on the topic of India's relationship with Israel, with one poster noting that the BJP was more pro-Israel than Congress.

A counterclaim was made that Congress had historically been more supportive of Palestine. The discussion was marked by various claims and counter-claims about the influence of Jewish groups and Israeli companies in Indian politics and society.

articles.title: Anon describes brutal scenario
articles.body:
Anon kicked off the discussion with a two-word post: 'Absolutely brutal.' One anon responded with a request for clarification, inquiring if the story was about 'a pajeeta who got fucked by a BWC.' Another anon provided a link to a 4chan thread, suggesting the story originated there.

Anon then shared a link to an xHamster video, jokingly suggesting it might be related to the story.

A respondent deemed the scenario 'illegal' and an 'insult to the National Flag.' Anon dismissed the significance of the story, stating it wouldn't 'matter much in the grand scheme of things.' Others made claims about the prevalence of interracial relationships in India, with one stating 'pajeets fuck white bitches left and right.' A discussion about the supposed illegality and offensiveness of the scenario ensued, with one participant labeling it 'disgusting and sub5.' Anon referenced 'fagyush,' a name that seemed to evoke a strong reaction.

The conversation also touched on the idea that Indians are often used as 'cumdumps' by low-status individuals abroad.

articles.title: Anon queries South Indian ancestry
articles.body: Anon opened the thread saying "Kek tf is happenung to retardjeets". Anon claimed "average genz male is 5’10 these days" and another anon countered they were 6’1. One anon asked how Africans reached South India without passing through North India, and another suggested Biharis are not Aryans. Anon also joked about Keralites, saying "saar keral numba one".

articles.title: Anon suggests creating a /gen/ board
articles.body:
Anon opened a thread asking if there should be a /gen/ board. One respondent agreed, saying /b/ gets boring and stagnant, and a /gen/ board would allow anons with actual hobbies to share and discuss meaningfully.

Another poster claimed to be the admin and jokingly offered a mod position to someone who said they'd 'suck their cock.' The admin later stated they would create a /gen/ board because many anons requested it, but would delete it if it stayed inactive.
